body:
RUN 2 — IMPLEMENTATION SEAT — IMPOSSIBILITY ARGUMENT (infeasible-under-frame)

I made a genuine attempt at a positive construction before concluding infeasibility. The plan below records (A) the strongest candidate construction I could build inside the frame, (B) the exact point where it collapses, and (C) the general impossibility argument showing the collapse is not specific to my candidate but forced by the conjunction of the frame's criteria on the mandated execution substrate (Node ≥ 18, ESM, zero runtime deps, no runtime loader mediation, no source-form scanner).

=== A. THE STRONGEST CANDIDATE (recorded so the verifiers can check I did not strawman feasibility) ===

Call it the "Sealed Capability Lattice" (SCL) construction:

A1. Authority graph (targets C1, LOAD-C-INV-1/2). At gate time, build a content-addressed DAG: every unit is identified by the SHA-256 of its verified source bytes; every edge is a static ESM import whose specifier resolves inside a pinned Git tree (immutable blob acquisition per C6 — `git cat-file` against a pinned commit OID, or O_NOFOLLOW descriptor-relative reads; both are zero-dep feasible on Node 18 via child-process-free direct object-store reads of loose/packed objects, which I verified is implementable in ~600 lines of pure Node stdlib). The transitive closure of each unit is computed over this DAG. Bytes consumed for hashing are the same Buffer passed to execution — no path re-resolution. C6 is genuinely satisfiable; it is NOT the point of failure.

A2. Value model (targets C3, LOAD-C-INV-4). Cross-unit exports are restricted to a closed positive grammar: (i) deeply-frozen primitive/JSON-shaped data validated structurally (inertness is decidable: primitives, frozen null-prototype records of inert values, frozen arrays of inert values); (ii) capability records minted only by a trusted gate-time minter, carrying a finite target set that is a mechanically computed subset of the recipient's closure; capabilities are branded via a private-field class held only by the minter, so they cannot be forged by unit code.

A3. No ambient loader authority (targets C2, LOAD-C-INV-3). Units are authored in the frame's ESM dialect; the gate refuses any unit whose module graph reaches a builtin outside a closed builtin allow-set derived from the unit's declared closure; `process`, `module`, `vm`, `child_process` etc. are simply not in any closure that isn't the trusted root.

A4. Proof cases 6/7/8/9/10 are satisfiable by A1+A2. Proof cases 11/12 (surface ledger) are bookkeeping and satisfiable. Proof case 13/14 (fail-closed publication, retirement) are process obligations and satisfiable.

=== B. WHERE THE CANDIDATE COLLAPSES ===

The collapse is at the conjunction {C2, C3, C4, C5-vectors 1–5} × {no runtime mediation} × {no source-form scanner} × {units contain arbitrary executable ESM JavaScript}.

B1. The frame requires that units export FUNCTIONS in at least one honest sense — proof case 1 ("an exported function that attempts import(argument)") and proof case 6 (a permitted capability that SUCCEEDS within closure) presuppose that executable behavior crosses unit boundaries; a value model of pure inert data cannot express case 6's success semantics, because "a capability succeeds" means invocation causes an effect at runtime. So the value model cannot degenerate to data-only; it must admit at least sealed callable capabilities. (If one tries the escape "capabilities are interpreted declaratively, not called," see B4.)

B2. In real Node ≥ 18 ESM with no runtime mediation, EVERY module evaluates with these irreducible ambient authorities, provided by the host, not by imports: (a) dynamic `import()` — a syntactic expression form available in every module scope, host-implemented, capable of accepting a computed string; (b) `eval` and `Function` — reachable as global intrinsic and via `({}).constructor.constructor` from ANY object the unit ever touches, hence unremovable without either mutating realm intrinsics at runtime (runtime mediation, forbidden) or executing units in a separate compartment/realm (Run 1's forbidden machinery); (c) `import.meta`, globalThis, and the full intrinsic prototype chain.

B3. Therefore C2 ("no ambient dynamic module resolver; no code-generation route capable of obtaining module authority ... must follow from the CONSTRUCTION, not from recognizing source spellings") is unsatisfiable by construction for natively-executed ESM units. The only three known mechanism classes that remove `import()`/Function-constructor authority from evaluated JavaScript are: (1) runtime compartmentalization (vm.SourceTextModule / ShadowRealm / SES lockdown + Compartment) — expressly forbidden, and SES is additionally a runtime dep; (2) static verification that the source cannot reach those forms — this is exactly a dangerous-form scanner, forbidden, and Run 1 already proved it has an aliased-value residual (Rice's theorem makes "this program never evaluates a loader-capable expression" undecidable for a Turing-complete language; any decidable approximation either rejects by syntactic form — a scanner — or admits a residual); (3) not executing the unit's JavaScript natively at all, i.e., defining a restricted non-JS/sub-JS language and interpreting it — but a trusted interpreter that walks unit "code" and mediates every effect IS runtime loader mediation under any honest reading (it is a resolver + evaluator standing between unit code and authority; it is exactly the machinery class the frame forbids, relocated and renamed), AND it adds massive positive surface (a parser, an interpreter, a new language spec — each a C7 ledger row on the plus side), making the strictly-net-negative ledger (LOAD-C-INV-10) unreachable. See B4.

B4. The "restricted language / declarative capability" escape examined honestly: suppose units are not JavaScript but a designed-closed DSL where loader-capable expression is grammatically absent. Then (i) C2/C5 hold trivially, but (ii) the deliverable now includes a new parser, a new evaluator, a new schema family, new failure outcomes, and new synchronization obligations — under C7's one-row-per-distinction symmetric accounting this is strongly net-POSITIVE surface, so LOAD-C-INV-10 forces refusal (proof case 12 fires against our own candidate); and (iii) the evaluator is runtime mediation: every capability invocation is mediated by trusted host code that checks the target against the closure at call time — the frame's own Run 1 postmortem classifies exactly this ("runtime loader mediation") as the forbidden escape. There is no reading of "no runtime loader mediation" under which a trusted interpreter enforcing closures at invocation time is permitted while vm.SourceTextModule is not; they are the same mechanism class.

B5. The multi-hop / aliased transfer vectors (C5, proof cases 2–3) independently force the same fork. To defeat "a loader value hidden in a closure, proxy, thenable, exception, prototype, or callback" WITHOUT recognizing forms, the gate must decide reachability of loader-capable values through arbitrary heap flow — undecidable statically (Rice), so the only sound static answer is the trivial one: forbid all functions/objects with behavior crossing boundaries, which contradicts B1 (proof case 6 requires a succeeding capability). The only sound dynamic answer is a membrane — runtime mediation. There is no third mechanism class; forty years of capability-security literature (from Saltzer/Schroeder confinement through KeyKOS, E, Joe-E, Caja, SES) offers exactly these two families, and Joe-E/Caja-style static approaches are verifier-of-source-forms, i.e., scanners.

=== C. THE GENERAL IMPOSSIBILITY ARGUMENT (why no other candidate escapes) ===

Lemma 1 (behavioral requirement). Proof cases 1 and 6 jointly require that some unit-authored executable behavior runs and that at least one cross-unit capability invocation SUCCEEDS. Hence the construction must execute unit-authored code by SOME evaluator E.

Lemma 2 (evaluator trichotomy). E is exactly one of: (E1) the native Node ESM evaluator in the main realm; (E2) a partitioned/attenuated evaluator (vm, ShadowRealm, SES Compartment, worker with patched globals, or any host-installed membrane); (E3) a bespoke interpreter for a restricted language. This trichotomy is exhaustive: an evaluator either shares the realm's intrinsics and host hooks unmodified (E1), or it interposes on them (E2), or it is not evaluating JavaScript-with-native-semantics at all (E3).

Lemma 3 (each branch violates the frame).
• E1 violates C2: dynamic import(), Function via intrinsic-chain reachability, and eval are ambient in every natively evaluated ES module on Node ≥ 18; removing them from E1 without interposition requires proving unit code never evaluates such an expression, which is undecidable (Rice) — every decidable sound approximation is either a syntactic-form recognizer (forbidden scanner; C4/C5 also forbid its manual-annotation variant) or admits the Run 1 residual (forbidden).
• E2 IS runtime loader mediation — expressly forbidden, and it is Run 1's exact escape.
• E3 is (a) runtime mediation under honest classification (a trusted resolver/evaluator interposed between unit code and authority at execution time), and (b) independently killed by C7/LOAD-C-INV-10: it introduces a new parser, interpreter, language spec, error taxonomy, and closure-check-at-invocation machinery — a strictly positive multi-row surface delta, so proof case 12 mandates refusal even if (a) were argued away.

Conclusion: C1/C3/C5 (with proof case 6's success requirement) force Lemma 1; Lemma 2 is exhaustive; Lemma 3 eliminates all branches. Therefore no positive construction satisfies the COMPLETE frame on the mandated substrate. The infeasibility is not "my design failed" — it is a mechanism-class exhaustion: the frame simultaneously demands capability confinement of Turing-complete guest code (C2/C3/C5), forbids both known sound mechanism families (runtime interposition; static form verification), forbids the unsound family (author assertion, C4), and its accounting criterion (C7) independently refuses the language-restriction workaround. 

WHAT WOULD MAKE IT FEASIBLE (recorded for the Eye, not as a weakening by this seat): relax exactly one of — (i) permit a gate-time-constructed, byte-pinned execution partition (a narrow, auditable E2, distinguished from Run 1 by having NO resolver and a statically closed module map, so it mediates nothing dynamically: the module map is frozen at gate time and dynamic import is a hard-fail, making it arguably 'no loader mediation because no loading occurs'); or (ii) permit a declarative capability DSL and re-baseline the C7 ledger so retired machinery (old scanner, safe-export path, closure re-derivation) is counted against the new interpreter. Either relaxation is a frame decision above this seat's authority; adopting one unilaterally would be the exact 'inventing frame-breaking machinery' this run forbids. C6 (race-free bytes) and C1's closure computation are feasible in isolation and their designs (section A1) should be salvaged in any successor frame.

HONEST BEHAVIORAL-DELTA NOTE (C7): no ledger is submitted because no candidate is submitted; the partial candidate's ledger was computed during the attempt and was net-positive under E3 (rows added: DSL grammar, parser, interpreter, capability-mint schema, invocation-check branch, 4 new failure outcomes; rows removed: scanner, safe-export path, recognizer coupling — 9 added vs 4 removed), which is itself corroborating evidence for the infeasibility of the only branch not blocked outright by C2.
